title: Generate returns structured output envelope
description:
**As a** report service, **I want** `generate()` to return the expected `Tuple[Optional[Dict], Optional[str]]`, **so that** the block can participate in report generation without error.

In this shell story, `generate()` returns a well-formed output dictionary with status information and an empty topics list, plus a log string. This proves the block can participate in the report generation lifecycle before any real dependencies (OpenSearch, embeddings, clustering) are wired up.

## Key Files

- Modify: `plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory.py`
- Modify: `plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory_test.py`

## Dependencies

Story plx-be9cda (Block registration and instantiation)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Feature: VectorTopicMemory generate returns structured output

  Scenario: Generate returns valid output tuple
    Given a VectorTopicMemory block with valid config
    And all external dependencies are mocked
    When generate() is awaited
    Then the first element is a dict with keys "status", "topics", "cluster_version", "summary"
    And "topics" is an empty list
    And the second element is a non-empty log string

  Scenario: Generate logs configuration extraction
    Given config containing data.source = "test-source"
    When generate() is awaited
    Then the log output contains "VectorTopicMemory"

  Scenario: Generate returns error when data config is missing
    Given config = {} with no "data" section
    When generate() is awaited
    Then the output dict contains an "errors" list
    And the error mentions "'data' configuration section is required"
